assertions.title: Moving background ships a vendored real Radiant asset

steps.action: Open the root page in a headed browser session and inspect the hero/background treatment.
steps.expected: A live Radiant-derived moving background is visible behind the hero and atlas surfaces.
steps.observed: A subtle blue animated background is visible behind the hero, graph cards, and lower proof section in headed mode.

steps.action: Inspect the rendered DOM for atmospheric canvas instances.
steps.expected: The shipped page exposes the actual moving shader layer rather than a placeholder.

steps.action: Audit the shipped implementation for provenance.
steps.expected: The visible background traces to a vendored Radiant asset path and source reference.
steps.observed: `src/components/visual/SiteAtmosphere.tsx` dynamically imports `HeroShaderCanvas`, and `src/components/visual/radiant-fluid-amber.glsl.ts` vendors the Radiant Fluid Amber shader with source reference `https://github.com/pbakaus/radiant/blob/main/static/fluid-amber.html`.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; console only showed the React DevTools recommendation and `[HMR] connected`.
evidence.network: Browser performance entries included `/_next/static/chunks/src_components_visual_HeroShaderCanvas_tsx_*.js` and `/grain.gif`; local asset loads only.

assertions.title: ASCII layer uses a real-time Hermes-derived canvas renderer

steps.action: Inspect the page visually and via DOM queries for any ASCII atmosphere layer.
steps.expected: A Hermes-derived real-time ASCII canvas renderer is present and materially visible.
steps.observed: No ASCII layer is visible anywhere on the page; the atmosphere is shader + film grain only.

steps.action: Count canvas and ASCII-related DOM nodes.
steps.expected: An ASCII-specific canvas renderer exists and the page does not fall back to a DOM text grid.
steps.observed: DOM inspection returned `allCanvasCount: 1` and `asciiDomNodeCount: 0`; the single canvas is the background shader, not an ASCII renderer.

steps.action: Audit the app source for shipped ASCII renderer code.
steps.expected: Hermes-style ASCII renderer files or imports remain if this assertion is satisfied.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; console only showed the React DevTools recommendation and `[HMR] connected`.
evidence.network: No ASCII-specific assets or renderer bundles were observed; the visible background assets were the shader chunk and `/grain.gif`.

assertions.issues: Fails against the current validation-contract wording because the shipped visual re-engineering intentionally removes ASCII entirely. This behavior aligns with VAL-VISUAL-028 and the mission guidance, but not with the stale Hermes/ASCII contract text.

assertions.title: Enhanced visual state is dramatically distinct from static fallback

steps.action: Capture the default enhanced desktop state in headed mode.
steps.expected: The enhanced state should look materially richer than fallback.
steps.observed: The enhanced state reports `heroVisualState: "enhanced"` with `heroMotionLayers: 1`, but the visible page remains very close to the fallback look.

steps.action: Relaunch the same page with `--disable-gpu,--disable-webgl` to force fallback and capture a fallback screenshot.
steps.expected: The fallback should be clearly flatter/static than the enhanced state.
steps.observed: The fallback reports `heroVisualState: "fallback"`, `heroMotionLayers: 0`, and `shaderCanvasCount: 0`, but the screenshot is visually near-identical to the enhanced state.

steps.action: Compare the enhanced screenshot to the fallback screenshot.
steps.expected: The comparison should show a dramatic visible change.
steps.observed: Pixel diff against the enhanced baseline found only 634 / 1,728,000 pixels different (0.04%), which is not a material visual distinction.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; console only showed the React DevTools recommendation and `[HMR] connected`.
evidence.network: Fallback run loaded the same local page assets but with WebGL disabled; no remote/network blocker was involved.

assertions.issues: The enhanced and forced-fallback states are almost indistinguishable in the rendered page. The underlying state flags change correctly, but the user-visible result is not dramatically distinct.

assertions.title: Headed browser validation shows visible background motion

steps.action: Run the page in headed mode and capture frame A before motion recording.
steps.expected: Visible background motion should be observable over time in the rendered page.
steps.observed: Frame A shows the subtle blue atmosphere, but no obvious motion is perceptible from the still image.

steps.action: Record a 2.5 second headed browser clip and capture frame B, then wait 5 more seconds and capture frame C.
steps.expected: The moving background should noticeably shift between frames and in the recording.
steps.observed: Frames A/B/C appear visually static to the eye; the recording does not surface clearly visible movement.

steps.action: Quantify the visual change between captured frames.
steps.expected: Inter-frame diffs should reflect visible motion rather than trace-level pixel noise.
steps.observed: Frame A vs B diff was 87 / 1,728,000 pixels (0.01%), and frame A vs C diff was 81 / 1,728,000 pixels (0.00%), which is too small to count as visible motion.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; console only showed the React DevTools recommendation and `[HMR] connected`.
evidence.network: Local asset loads only; no network issue blocked animation.

assertions.issues: In headed validation the background motion is too faint to be visibly confirmed. The page exposes an enhanced state, but the rendered motion is not perceptible enough to satisfy the assertion.

steps.action: Inspect the full rendered page for terminal-style ASCII overlays, box-drawing grids, or unicode decoration.
steps.expected: No ASCII layers or text-grid background elements remain anywhere on the page.
steps.observed: No ASCII or terminal-grid decoration is visible in the hero, atlas cards, proof section, or footer.

steps.action: Query the DOM for ASCII-related selectors and box-drawing characters.
steps.expected: No ASCII DOM hooks or box-drawing text should remain.
steps.observed: DOM inspection returned `asciiDomNodeCount: 0`, `boxDrawingCount: 0`, and no `<pre>`-based background layer.

steps.action: Audit source files for remaining ASCII imports/references.
steps.expected: No `AsciiCanvasRenderer`, `AsciiBackground`, or ASCII-related imports remain in shipped source.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; console only showed the React DevTools recommendation and `[HMR] connected`.
evidence.network: Only the local page assets, shader chunk, and `/grain.gif` loaded; no ASCII assets were observed.

assertions.title: Single shader site-wide atmosphere with film grain

steps.action: Inspect the rendered page at the hero and lower sections.
steps.expected: A single subtle site-wide shader and low-opacity film grain remain visible without competing with content.
steps.observed: A subdued blue atmosphere and fine grain texture remain visible across the hero, atlas, and lower proof cards while content stays readable.

steps.action: Inspect DOM/style data for atmosphere layers.
steps.expected: Exactly one shader instance is present site-wide, plus one film grain overlay with lighten blend and 5% opacity.
steps.observed: DOM inspection returned `shaderCanvasCount: 1`, `allCanvasCount: 1`, and film grain styles `mixBlendMode: "lighten"`, `opacity: "0.05"`, `backgroundImage: ".../grain.gif"`.

steps.action: Audit the implementation structure for duplicate shader usage.
steps.expected: The site atmosphere owns the only shader instance and applies the grain overlay globally.
steps.observed: `src/components/visual/SiteAtmosphere.tsx` imports `HeroShaderCanvas` and `FilmGrainOverlay`, renders `<HeroShaderCanvas />` once, and `src/app/globals.css` defines `.site-atmosphere-shader { opacity: 0.25; }` and `.film-grain-overlay { mix-blend-mode: lighten; opacity: 0.05; }`.

evidence.consoleErrors: none; console only showed the React DevTools recommendation and `[HMR] connected`.
evidence.network: Browser performance entries included `/grain.gif` and the local `HeroShaderCanvas` chunk; only one atmosphere canvas was present in DOM.

frictions.description: `VAL-VISUAL-016` and `VAL-VISUAL-018` still use stale ASCII/Hermes contract wording, while the approved visual re-engineering guidance removes ASCII entirely.
frictions.resolution: Reported the literal assertion outcomes and separately documented the actual shipped behavior: no ASCII remains, one shader + film grain only.

frictions.description: `agent-browser network requests` did not capture passive asset loads for this session.
frictions.resolution: Used `performance.getEntriesByType("resource")` inside the browser to confirm local asset loads including `/grain.gif` and the `HeroShaderCanvas` chunk.

frictions.description: Fallback comparison required a separate browser relaunch with WebGL disabled to force the degraded state.
frictions.resolution: Reran the same page in a headed session with `--disable-gpu,--disable-webgl`, captured `VAL-VISUAL-017-static-fallback-state.png`, and compared it against the enhanced baseline.

summary: Tested 6 assertions. Passed: VAL-VISUAL-015, VAL-VISUAL-028, VAL-VISUAL-029. Failed: VAL-VISUAL-016 (stale ASCII renderer contract does not match the intentionally ASCII-free shipped implementation), VAL-VISUAL-017 (enhanced and fallback states are not materially distinct), VAL-VISUAL-018 (headed browser motion is too faint to visibly confirm).
